title: Guide Cloning

Water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pytrp","text":"4. Oligos","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dk1ka","text":"5. BbsI-HF","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"r4stn","text":"6. steps: Thaw deep well plates of paired oligos in a centrifuge pre-cooled at 4C at 500g for 20 minutes . If oligos are in separate tubes, pair Watson (W) and Crick (C) oligo tubes. A B C 1 Reagent 1X (10 uL reaction) 100X (for full 96-well plate) 2 T4 DNA Ligase Buffer 1uL 100 uL 3 T4 Polynucleotide Kinase (PNK) 0.5 uL 50 uL 4 Water 6.5 uL 650 uL 5 Subtotal 8 uL 800 uL 6 Oligos 1uL of each oligo 2uL if paired in well X

Prepare phosphoannealing master mix on ice Thaw T4 ligase buffer on ice Do not use T4 PNK Buffer, use T4 Ligase buffer Do not use T4 Ligase enzyme, use T4 PNK Keep T4 PNK at -20C in a cool block Add buffer first, then enzyme, then mix with water volume
Dispense 8 uL of mix to each well of a 96-well plate or sets of 8-strip tubes
Transfer 2 uL of 100uM paired oligos into each well, or 1 uL each of 100uM oligos from individual W and C tubes
Seal with foil seal and briefly spin down
Use Phosphoannealing Oligos protocol on Eppendorf thermal cyclers A B C 1 37C 30min 95C 5min Ramp to 25C, -5C per min
Dilute 1 uL of phosphoannealing reaction into 199 uL water in a new 96-well plate, mixing thoroughly

Prepare Goldengate master mix on ice A B C 1 Reagent 1X (10 uL reaction) 100X 2 T4 DNA Ligase Buffer 1 uL 100 uL 3 T4 DNA Ligase 0.5 uL 50 uL 4 BbsI-HF 0.5 uL 50 uL 5 PX45-sfGFP 6 (50-100 ng/uL) 1 uL 100 uL 7 Water 6 uL 600 uL 8 Subtotal 9 uL 900 uL 9 1:200 diluted oligos 1 uL X Thaw T4 ligase buffer on ice Do not use T4 PNK enzyme, use T4 Ligase enzyme Keep T4 ligase and BbsI-HF at -20C in a cool block Add buffer first, then enzymes, then plasmid, then mix with water volume
Dispense 9 uL of Goldengate master mix to a new 96-well plate
Transfer 1 uL of diluted oligo duplexes to each well and mix thoroughly
Seal with foil seal and briefly spin down

Use BbsI T4lig GG protocol on Eppendorf thermal cyclers A B C D E 1 50X 1X 1X Hold 2 37 C (cut) 16 C (ligate) 50 C (extra cut) 80 C (inactivate) 10 C 3 1:00 min 1:00 min 30:00 min 5:00 min Hold 37 C (cut) for 1:00 min, 16 C (ligate) for 1:00 min, 50 C (extra cut) for 30:00 min, 80 C (inactivate) for 5:00 min, then hold at 10 C.
Thaw [# of samples / 10] vials of Stellar Competent E. Coli on ice for 10 minutes 1 vial contains slightly over 100 uL of cells
Preheat thermal cycler to 42 C Preheat LB + Amp agar plates in 37 C incubator Preheat LB to 37 C (optional)
Dispense 10 uL of cells to each well of a new 96-well plate
Transfer 1 uL of each goldengate mix to each well of cells Do not mix by pipetting, but ensure that the mix has been added to the middle of the cells, gently tap the side of the plate to mix
Seal with fold seal, do not spin down
Incubate cells on ice for additional 5 minutes
Heat-shock plate on thermal cycler with lid-open for 30 seconds
Incubate cells on ice for additional 2 minutes
Add 40 uL LB (20-37 C) to each well and mix
Add 45 uL LB to 2 new 96-well plates
Transfer 5 uL LB + Cells to dilution plate 1 and mix
Transfer 5 uL diluted LB + Cells from dilution plate 1 to dilution plate 2 and mix

Plate 5 uL of each well from dilution plate 2 on a new rectangular agar plate, or plate 50 uL on separate 10 cm agar plates Allow rectangular agar plate to dry underneath open flame until liquid has absorbed into agar Glass beads can spread media on 10 cm agar plates with no need for drying
Plate 2 uL of each well from dilution plate 2 on a second new rectangular agar plate
Grow overnight in 37 C incubator
Prepare a deep well plate for overnight growth Mix 50 mL Terrific Broth with 50 uL Carbenicillin Dispense 500 uL to each well of a new deep-well plate
Place agar plate with appropriate cell density on blue light block

Use pipette tip to pick non-GFP colonies from plate Drop tip in corresponding well of media-filled deep-well plate Colonies can also be picked and dropped immediately into 60 mL of Terrific Broth + 60 uL of Carbenicillin for overnight incubation if a Maxi-Prep is intended the following day.
Grow overnight at 37 C

The following day, prepare glycerol stocks Mix 500uL of 50% glycerol in water with each well in deep well plate Freeze glycerol stocks at -80 C. Glycerol stocks can be scraped with a pipette tip and dropped into 60 mL of Terrific Broth + 60 uL of Carbenicillin for overnight growth.
Centrifuge 50 mL of overnight growth at 3000 - 4000 rc f for 20 - 30 minutes Media should be clear and pellet should be large at bottom of conical tube
Perform Zymo Maxi-Prep according to the manufacturer protocol Elute with 400 uL 50 C water, not elution buffer Proceed with endotoxin removal
Quant Plasmid Prep with Qubit Broad Range kit

Send approx 200 ng in 10 uL volume + 5 uL 5 uM U6 primer (5' GACTATCATATGCTTACCGT 3') to Genewiz (or equivalent Sanger sequencing service) for sequence verification Store the prepared plasmid in the ‘awaiting confirmation’ sgRNA box.
The next day, upload sequences to Benchling

Use Benchling “Attach Primers” feature to automatically tag Watson strand sgRNA oligo sequences. If the Sanger trace is poor quality, resequence If there are visible BbsI cut sites in the sequence, dispose of the plasmid immediately If there are no visible BbsI cut sites in the sequence, but the sequence has not been autotagged by the Attach Primers feature, please confirm the sequence by looking at other documents and spreadsheets and create a new Primer or Annotation for the correct guide sequence. Only print a detailed label that references target and concentration if Sequence confirmed Acceptable concentration for transfection (typically �3e500 ng / uL)
creator: Nahum Smith
